title: రాముడి దయ, కాకసురుడి మార్పు
moral: చిన్న తప్పు చేసినా, నిజమైన పశ్చాత్తాపంతో క్షమ కోరితే, దయ తప్పక లభిస్తుంది.
main_character: Rama: a young prince with dark blue-black (shyama) complexion, tall and broad-shouldered, long arms reaching to his knees (Ajanabahu). During forest exile: wearing saffron-orange bark cloth (valkala), matted hair tied in a topknot (jata-mukuta), carrying his curved bow Kodanda and a quiver of arrows over his shoulder. Lotus-shaped eyes with a gentle reddish tinge at the corners. Noble, calm expression. Always the same character across every scene.
antagonist: A common forest crow, appearing mischievous at first, then terrified, and finally humbled. It is revealed to be Jayanta, Indra's son, in crow form. Always the same character across every scene.
setting: A serene forest clearing in Panchavati, with ancient trees and a small, clear stream nearby. The air is filled with the scent of wild flowers and damp earth. Same location across every scene.

scenes.text: పంచవటిలోని పర్ణశాలలో, రాముడు తన భుజంపై సీతమ్మ తల వాల్చుకుని ప్రశాంతంగా కూర్చున్నారు. చల్లటి గాలికి ఆకులు మెల్లగా కదిలాయి — అడవి అంతా నిశ్శబ్దంగా ఉంది. పక్కనే లక్ష్మణుడు తన విల్లు పట్టుకుని, వారిని కంటికి రెప్పలా కాపాడుతున్నాడు. రాముడు సీతమ్మను ప్రేమగా చూస్తూ, మెల్లగా ఆమె కేశాలను సవరించాడు. “సీతా, విశ్రాంతి తీసుకో,” అని ఆయన గొంతులో మృదుత్వం నిండి ఉంది. అప్పుడే, ఒక కాకి ఆ చెట్టు కొమ్మ మీద వాలి, వారిని ఆత్రంగా చూసింది — దాని కళ్ళు మెరుస్తున్నాయి.
scenes.image_prompt: The man gently caresses the woman's hair as she rests her head on his shoulder. A crow perches on a tree branch nearby, its eyes gleaming as it watches them intently. SUPPORTING CHARACTER also in this scene: Sita: a graceful young woman (mid-twenties) with a fair golden complexion and long dark hair adorned with jasmine flowers. Wearing a silk saree in deep red or golden hues, with traditional gold ornaments: necklace, bangles, nose ring, and earrings. Eyes reflecting deep wisdom and quiet dignity. Serene, composed expression. Soft afternoon sun dappling through dense forest canopy, creating warm patches of light and cool shadows, peaceful and serene.
scenes.scene_visual: The man gently caresses the woman's hair as she rests her head on his shoulder. A crow perches on a tree branch nearby, its eyes gleaming as it watches them intently.
scenes.text_te_en: Panchavatiloni Parnashaalalo, Raamudu Tana Bhujampai Sitamma Tala Vaalchukuni Prashaantangaa Kurchunnaaru. Challati Gaaliki Aakulu Mellagaa Kadilaayi — Adavi Antaa Nishshabdangaa Undi. Pakkane Lakshmanudu Tana Villu Pattukuni, Vaarini Kantiki Reppalaa Kaapaadutunnaadu. Raamudu Sitammanu Premagaa Chustu, Mellagaa Aame Keshaalanu Savarinchaadu. “Sitaa, Vishraanti Tisuko,” Ani Aayana Gontulo Mrridutvam Nindi Undi. Appude, Oka Kaaki Aa Chettu Komma Mida Vaali, Vaarini Aatrangaa Chusindi — Daani Kallu Merustunnaayi.
scenes.text_en: In their simple home in Panchavati, Ramudu sat peacefully with Seethamma's head resting on his shoulder. The leaves gently swayed in the cool breeze — the whole forest was silent. Nearby, Lakshmanudu held his bow, watching over them carefully. Ramudu looked at Seethamma lovingly and gently stroked her hair. "Seetha, take some rest," he said, his voice full of tenderness. Just then, a crow landed on a tree branch and watched them eagerly — its eyes were shining.

scenes.text: ఆ కాకి, ఉన్నట్టుండి రెక్కలు టపటపలాడించింది — ఒక క్షణం కూడా ఆగకుండా. సీతమ్మ తన కళ్ళు మూసుకుని, ప్రశాంతంగా ఉన్న సమయంలో, ఆ కాకి తన ముక్కుతో ఆమె పాదాన్ని ఒక్కసారి పొడిచింది. ఆమె ఒక్కసారిగా కుడికాలు లాక్కుంది. “ఆఁ!” అని ఆమె పెదవుల నుండి చిన్న బాధా ధ్వని వచ్చింది. రాముడు క్షణాల్లో కళ్ళు తెరిచి, సీతమ్మ వైపు చూశాడు — ఆమె ముఖంలో బాధ కనిపించింది. ఆ కాకి తన రెక్కలు గట్టిగా కొట్టుకుంటూ, దూరంగా ఎగిరింది — దాని అల్లరి కళ్ళు రాముడి వైపు చూశాయి.
scenes.image_prompt: The crow suddenly pecks the sleeping woman's foot with its beak, causing her to flinch and pull back her leg. A pained expression crosses the woman's face as the crow flaps its wings vigorously and flies away, its mischievous eyes looking back at the man. Also in this scene: A common forest crow, appearing mischievous at first, then terrified, and finally humbled. It is revealed to be Jayanta, Indra's son, in crow form. Always the same character across every scene. SUPPORTING CHARACTER also in this scene: Sita: a graceful young woman (mid-twenties) with a fair golden complexion and long dark hair adorned with jasmine flowers. Wearing a silk saree in deep red or golden hues, with traditional gold ornaments: necklace, bangles, nose ring, and earrings. Eyes reflecting deep wisdom and quiet dignity. Serene, composed expression. The dappled sunlight remains, but a sudden shadow falls over Sita and Rama's faces as the crow approaches, creating a subtle shift to unease.
scenes.scene_visual: The crow suddenly pecks the sleeping woman's foot with its beak, causing her to flinch and pull back her leg. A pained expression crosses the woman's face as the crow flaps its wings vigorously and flies away, its mischievous eyes looking back at the man.
scenes.text_te_en: Aa Kaaki, Unnattundi Rekkalu Tapatapalaadinchindi — Oka Kshanam Kudaa Aagakundaa. Sitamma Tana Kallu Musukuni, Prashaantangaa Unna Samayanlo, Aa Kaaki Tana Mukkuto Aame Paadaanni Okkasaari Podichindi. Aame Okkasaarigaa Kudikaalu Laakkundi. “Aa.N!” Ani Aame Pedavula Nundi Chinna Baadhaa Dhvani Vachchindi. Raamudu Kshanaallo Kallu Terichi, Sitamma Vaipu Chushaadu — Aame Mukhanlo Baadha Kanipinchindi. Aa Kaaki Tana Rekkalu Gattigaa Kottukuntu, Durangaa Egirindi — Daani Allari Kallu Raamudi Vaipu Chushaayi.
scenes.text_en: Suddenly, that crow flapped its wings loudly — without stopping for even a moment. While Seethamma had her eyes closed and was peaceful, the crow pecked her foot once with its beak. She quickly pulled back her right foot. "Ah!" a small sound of pain came from her lips. Ramudu opened his eyes in an instant and looked at Seethamma — pain was visible on her face. The crow flapped its wings strongly and flew away — its mischievous eyes looked towards Ramudu.

scenes.text: సీతమ్మ కంటి నుండి జారిన చిన్న నీటి బిందువును చూసిన రాముడి మనసు బాధతో మెలితిరిగింది. ఆయన తన చేతిలోకి ఒక చిన్న దర్భ గడ్డిని తీసుకున్నాడు — అది సాధారణ గడ్డిలా లేదు. రాముడు ఆ దర్భను తన బొటనవేలు, చూపుడువేలు మధ్య పట్టుకుని, ఏదో శక్తివంతమైన మంత్రాన్ని మనసులో ఉచ్చరించాడు. ఒక్కసారిగా ఆ దర్భ — దివ్యమైన కాంతి తో మెరిసింది. “ధర్మ సంరక్షణ!” అని రాముడు గంభీరంగా పలికాడు, ఆ గడ్డిని విల్లు నుండి వదిలాడు. ఆ బ్రహ్మాస్త్రం కాకిని వెంబడిస్తూ, ఆకాశంలోకి దూసుకుపోయింది — వెనుక ఒక బంగారు కాంతి రేఖను వదులుతూ.
scenes.image_prompt: The man holds a small blade of sacred grass between his thumb and forefinger, then releases it from his bow. The blade of grass glows with a divine light as it shoots into the sky, leaving a golden trail behind it. Also in this scene: A common forest crow, appearing mischievous at first, then terrified, and finally humbled. It is revealed to be Jayanta, Indra's son, in crow form. Always the same character across every scene. A sudden bright, golden-white light emanates from Rama's hand, illuminating the forest clearing, contrasting with the darkening shadows as the crow flies away.
scenes.scene_visual: The man holds a small blade of sacred grass between his thumb and forefinger, then releases it from his bow. The blade of grass glows with a divine light as it shoots into the sky, leaving a golden trail behind it.
scenes.text_te_en: Sitamma Kanti Nundi Jaarina Chinna Niti Binduvunu Chusina Raamudi Manasu Baadhato Melitirigindi. Aayana Tana Chetiloki Oka Chinna Darbha Gaddini Tisukunnaadu — Adi Saadhaarana Gaddilaa Ledu. Raamudu Aa Darbhanu Tana Botanavelu, Chupuduvelu Madhya Pattukuni, Edo Shaktivantamaina Mantraanni Manasulo Uchcharinchaadu. Okkasaarigaa Aa Darbha — Divyamaina Kaanti To Merisindi. “Dharma Sanrakshana!” Ani Raamudu Gambhirangaa Palikaadu, Aa Gaddini Villu Nundi Vadilaadu. Aa Brahmaastram Kaakini Vembadistu, Aakaashanloki Dusukupoyindi — Venuka Oka Bangaaru Kaanti Rekhanu Vadulutu.
scenes.text_en: Seeing the small tear that fell from Seethamma's eye, Ramudu's heart twisted with pain. He took a small blade of Darbha grass into his hand — it was not like ordinary grass. Ramudu held the Darbha between his thumb and forefinger and chanted a powerful mantra in his mind. Suddenly, that Darbha shone with a divine light. "Protection of Dharma!" Ramudu declared solemnly, and released the grass from his bow. That divine arrow chased the crow, soaring into the sky — leaving a golden trail of light behind it.

scenes.text: ఆ దర్భాస్త్రం తన వెనుక వెంబడిస్తుందని గ్రహించిన కాకి రెక్కలు వేగంగా కొట్టుకుంటూ, భయంతో ఆకాశంలోకి ఎగిరింది. అది దేవలోకాలన్నీ తిరిగింది — బ్రహ్మ లోకంలోకి, శివ లోకంలోకి, చివరికి విష్ణు లోకంలోకి కూడా దూసుకుపోయింది. ప్రతిచోటా దాని రెక్కల వేగం పెరిగింది, కానీ అస్త్రం వేగం తగ్గలేదు. “ఎవ్వరూ నన్ను కాపాడలేరా?” అని ఆ కాకి భయంతో అరిచింది, గాలిలో దాని ఈకలు రాలాయి. ఎక్కడా శరణం దొరకదని గ్రహించిన కాకసురుడు — తన ప్రాణాలు అరచేతిలో పట్టుకుని, తిరిగి రాముడి వైపు పరుగు తీశాడు.
scenes.image_prompt: The crow frantically beats its wings, soaring through the sky in terror as the glowing weapon relentlessly pursues it. Feathers fall from its body as it cries out in fear, realizing there is no escape. Also in this scene: A common forest crow, appearing mischievous at first, then terrified, and finally humbled. It is revealed to be Jayanta, Indra's son, in crow form. Always the same character across every scene. A dynamic sequence of various landscapes, from bright skies to dark caverns, all with the persistent, glowing trail of the Brahmastra, creating a sense of urgency and relentless pursuit.
scenes.scene_visual: The crow frantically beats its wings, soaring through the sky in terror as the glowing weapon relentlessly pursues it. Feathers fall from its body as it cries out in fear, realizing there is no escape.
scenes.text_te_en: Aa Darbhaastram Tana Venuka Vembadistundani Grahinchina Kaaki Rekkalu Vegangaa Kottukuntu, Bhayanto Aakaashanloki Egirindi. Adi Devalokaalanni Tirigindi — Brahma Lokanloki, Shiva Lokanloki, Chivariki Vishnu Lokanloki Kudaa Dusukupoyindi. Pratichotaa Daani Rekkala Vegam Perigindi, Kaani Astram Vegam Taggaledu. “Evvaru Nannu Kaapaadaleraa?” Ani Aa Kaaki Bhayanto Arichindi, Gaalilo Daani Ikalu Raalaayi. Ekkadaa Sharanam Dorakadani Grahinchina Kaakasurudu — Tana Praanaalu Arachetilo Pattukuni, Tirigi Raamudi Vaipu Parugu Tishaadu.
scenes.text_en: Realizing that the Darbhastra was chasing it, the crow flapped its wings quickly and flew into the sky in fear. It flew through all the divine worlds — into Brahma Loka, Shiva Loka, and finally even Vishnu Loka. Everywhere, its wing speed increased, but the arrow's speed did not decrease. "Can no one save me?" the crow cried out in fear, its feathers falling in the air. Realizing that it could find no refuge anywhere, Kakasurudu — fearing for his life — rushed back towards Ramudu.

scenes.text: అన్ని లోకాలు తిరిగి, చివరికి అలసిపోయిన కాకసురుడు, రాముడి పాదాల చెంత వాలాడు — అతని రెక్కలు వణికాయి. అది సాధారణ కాకి కాదు, ఇంద్రుడి కుమారుడు జయంతుడు. భయంతో, పశ్చాత్తాపంతో తల వంచి, రాముడి ముందు నిలబడ్డాడు. అతని ముఖంలో భయం, కళ్ళల్లో క్షమించమని వేడుకుంటున్న దీనత్వం స్పష్టంగా కనిపించాయి. “కా... కా... కా...” అని బలహీనంగా అరిచాడు, తన తప్పును ఒప్పుకున్నట్లు. రాముడు ప్రశాంతంగా అతని వైపు చూశాడు — జయంతుడు నేల మీద పూర్తిగా వాలిపోయాడు.
scenes.image_prompt: The exhausted crow-like figure collapses at the man's feet, bowing its head in fear and remorse. Its wings tremble, and a look of desperate pleading is visible in its eyes. Also in this scene: A common forest crow, appearing mischievous at first, then terrified, and finally humbled. It is revealed to be Jayanta, Indra's son, in crow form. Always the same character across every scene. The forest clearing is now bathed in a soft, warm, late afternoon glow, highlighting the crow's small form at Rama's feet, symbolizing a moment of quiet humility and impending mercy.
scenes.scene_visual: The exhausted crow-like figure collapses at the man's feet, bowing its head in fear and remorse. Its wings tremble, and a look of desperate pleading is visible in its eyes.
scenes.text_te_en: Anni Lokaalu Tirigi, Chivariki Alasipoyina Kaakasurudu, Raamudi Paadaala Chenta Vaalaadu — Atani Rekkalu Vanikaayi. Adi Saadhaarana Kaaki Kaadu, Indrudi Kumaarudu Jayantudu. Bhayanto, Pashchaattaapanto Tala Vanchi, Raamudi Mundu Nilabaddaadu. Atani Mukhanlo Bhayan, Kallallo Kshaminchamani Vedukuntunna Dinatvam Spashtangaa Kanipinchaayi. “Kaa... Kaa... Kaa...” Ani Balahinangaa Arichaadu, Tana Tappunu Oppukunnatlu. Raamudu Prashaantangaa Atani Vaipu Chushaadu — Jayantudu Nela Mida Purtigaa Vaalipoyaadu.
scenes.text_en: After circling all the worlds, finally, the tired Kakasurudu fell at Ramudu's feet — his wings trembled. It was not an ordinary crow, but Jayantudu, the son of Indra. With fear and repentance, he bowed his head and stood before Ramudu. Fear was clearly visible on his face, and in his eyes, a piteous plea for forgiveness. "Caw... Caw... Caw..." he cried weakly, as if admitting his mistake. Ramudu looked at him calmly — Jayantudu completely collapsed onto the ground.

scenes.text: రాముడు జయంతుడిని చూసి, అతని పశ్చాత్తాపాన్ని అర్థం చేసుకున్నాడు. ఆయన ముఖంలో కరుణ తొణికిసలాడింది, కానీ ధర్మం కూడా పాటించాలి. రాముడు ఆ దర్భాస్త్రాన్ని ఆపలేదు, కానీ దాని దిశను మార్చాడు — అది జయంతుడి కుడి కన్ను వైపు దూసుకుపోయింది. ఆ క్షణంలో — ఒక దివ్యమైన కాంతి మెరిసింది. “శరణాగతిని మన్నించాను,” అని రాముడు మృదువుగా అన్నాడు, జయంతుడికి ఒక కన్ను లేకుండా చేశాడు. జయంతుడు, ఒక కన్ను కోల్పోయినా, కృతజ్ఞతతో రాముడికి నమస్కరించాడు — తన ప్రాణం నిలిచిందని ఊపిరి పీల్చుకున్నాడు.
scenes.image_prompt: The man redirects the glowing weapon, and it shoots towards the crow-like figure's right eye. A divine light flashes at that moment, and the crow-like figure bows in gratitude despite losing an eye. Also in this scene: A common forest crow, appearing mischievous at first, then terrified, and finally humbled. It is revealed to be Jayanta, Indra's son, in crow form. Always the same character across every scene. A brief, intense burst of golden-white divine light around Rama and the crow, quickly fading to a serene, warm, golden hour light, signifying divine intervention and peace.
scenes.scene_visual: The man redirects the glowing weapon, and it shoots towards the crow-like figure's right eye. A divine light flashes at that moment, and the crow-like figure bows in gratitude despite losing an eye.
scenes.text_te_en: Raamudu Jayantudini Chusi, Atani Pashchaattaapaanni Artham Chesukunnaadu. Aayana Mukhanlo Karuna Tonikisalaadindi, Kaani Dharmam Kudaa Paatinchaali. Raamudu Aa Darbhaastraanni Aapaledu, Kaani Daani Dishanu Maarchaadu — Adi Jayantudi Kudi Kannu Vaipu Dusukupoyindi. Aa Kshananlo — Oka Divyamaina Kaanti Merisindi. “Sharanaagatini Manninchaanu,” Ani Raamudu Mrriduvugaa Annaadu, Jayantudiki Oka Kannu Lekundaa Cheshaadu. Jayantudu, Oka Kannu Kolpoyinaa, Krritajnyatato Raamudiki Namaskarinchaadu — Tana Praanam Nilichindani Upiri Pilchukunnaadu.
scenes.text_en: Ramudu looked at Jayantudu and understood his repentance. Compassion shone on his face, but Dharma (righteousness) also had to be upheld. Ramudu did not stop the Darbhastra, but changed its direction — it rushed towards Jayantudu's right eye. At that moment — a divine light shone. "I have forgiven your surrender," Ramudu said softly, making Jayantudu lose one eye. Jayantudu, even after losing one eye, bowed to Ramudu with gratitude — he breathed a sigh of relief that his life was saved.

scenes.text: జయంతుడు, ఒక కన్ను కోల్పోయి, జీవిత పాఠం నేర్చుకున్నాడు — అతడి మనసు మారింది. అతను నెమ్మదిగా రెక్కలు విప్పుకుని, రాముడికి మరోసారి శిరస్సు వంచి, ఆకాశంలోకి ఎగిరిపోయాడు. అల్లరి, అహంకారం పోయి, అతని చూపులో వినయం, కృతజ్ఞత నిండిపోయాయి. అడవిలో — ఒకే ఒక కాకి కూత వినిపించింది, అది రాముడి దయను కీర్తించినట్లు ఉంది. ఆ రోజు నుండి, జయంతుడు ఎప్పుడూ ఎవరికీ హాని చేయలేదు — తన జీవితాంతం రాముడిని స్మరించుకున్నాడు.
scenes.image_prompt: The one-eyed crow-like figure slowly spreads its wings, bows its head once more to the man, and flies into the sky. Its gaze, now filled with humility and gratitude, reflects its changed heart. Also in this scene: A common forest crow, appearing mischievous at first, then terrified, and finally humbled. It is revealed to be Jayanta, Indra's son, in crow form. Always the same character across every scene. The sun begins to set, casting long, warm, orange-pink shadows across the forest, creating a peaceful and reflective atmosphere as the crow flies off into the distance.
scenes.scene_visual: The one-eyed crow-like figure slowly spreads its wings, bows its head once more to the man, and flies into the sky. Its gaze, now filled with humility and gratitude, reflects its changed heart.
scenes.text_te_en: Jayantudu, Oka Kannu Kolpoyi, Jivita Paatham Nerchukunnaadu — Atadi Manasu Maarindi. Atanu Nemmadigaa Rekkalu Vippukuni, Raamudiki Marosaari Shirassu Vanchi, Aakaashanloki Egiripoyaadu. Allari, Ahankaaram Poyi, Atani Chupulo Vinayan, Krritajnyata Nindipoyaayi. Adavilo — Oke Oka Kaaki Kuta Vinipinchindi, Adi Raamudi Dayanu Kirtinchinatlu Undi. Aa Roju Nundi, Jayantudu Eppudu Evariki Haani Cheyaledu — Tana Jivitaantam Raamudini Smarinchukunnaadu.
scenes.text_en: Jayantudu, having lost one eye, learned a life lesson — his heart changed. He slowly spread his wings, bowed his head to Ramudu once more, and flew into the sky. His mischief and arrogance were gone, and his gaze was filled with humility and gratitude. In the forest — only one crow's caw was heard, as if praising Ramudu's kindness. From that day on, Jayantudu never harmed anyone — he remembered Ramudu throughout his life.

supporting_characters.description: Sita: a graceful young woman (mid-twenties) with a fair golden complexion and long dark hair adorned with jasmine flowers. Wearing a silk saree in deep red or golden hues, with traditional gold ornaments: necklace, bangles, nose ring, and earrings. Eyes reflecting deep wisdom and quiet dignity. Serene, composed expression. Always the same character across every scene.

Lakshmana: a young prince (mid-twenties) with a warm golden-brown complexion, muscular and alert — slightly shorter and more compact than Rama. During forest exile: wearing saffron-orange bark cloth (valkala), matted hair tied in a topknot, carrying his bow and full quiver, always standing in a protective, watchful posture. He is NOT old, NOT a monk, NOT grey-haired — he is a vigorous young warrior-prince. Determined, loyal expression; the devoted younger brother. Always the same character across every scene.

quality_scores.notes: This story boasts a compelling narrative arc and flawless grammar, making it a strong retelling of a classic tale. However, its primary weakness is the tendency to state emotions directly rather than showing them through character actions or reactions. To elevate this to an award-worthy piece, the author must revise instances where emotions are told (e.g., 'ముఖంలో బాధ కనిపించింది,' 'భయంతో అరిచింది') into vivid, sensory descriptions. Additionally, simplifying some formal vocabulary would significantly enhance the oral warmth and direct engagement for a child listener.

moral_te_en: Chinna Tappu Chesinaa, Nijamaina Pashchaattaapanto Kshama Korite, Daya Tappaka Labhistundi.
moral_en: Even if you make a small mistake, if you ask for forgiveness with true regret, kindness will surely be granted.
